TaskDescription: Longitudinal BCI training with inter-subject transfer learning. Subjects performed left/right hand MI with bar-feedback and car racing tasks across 6 sessions on separate days. Two groups: Generic Recentering (GR, N=9) and Personally Assisted Recentering (PAR, N=9). Trial duration: 5.0s, cue: 1.5s, imagery: 5.0s.
Instructions: Subjects were instructed to mentally rehearse the kinesthetics, not the visualization, of left- or right-hand movements without overtly causing any muscle contraction.
InstitutionName: The University of Texas at Austin
Description: Multi-session longitudinal MI training dataset with 18 BCI-naive subjects over 6 sessions. Demonstrates that inter-subject transfer learning from a single expert promotes acquisition of individual BCI skills via unsupervised domain adaptation.
